title: scurrula parasitica var. laevigata
parts: Scurrula parasitica var. laevigata has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and stems, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Scurrula parasitica var. laevigata are rich in alkaloids like scuruline, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial properties. The roots of the plant are known to contain flavonoids like kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, which have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The stems of Scurrula parasitica var. laevigata also contain iridoids, specifically scurulin and laevigatin, which have shown promise in inhibiting the growth of certain microorganisms.
side_effects: Scurrula parasitica var. laevigata can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may lead to headaches, dizziness, and even liver damage in severe cases. It may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, resulting in adverse reactions. In rare instances, the plant's sap may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and dermatitis if not handled carefully.
